content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

SUPERSEDED IN PART
BY 1998 AMENDMENTS TO
REGULATION 18530

October 27, 1992


Steve Lipton, Esq.
Musick, Peeler, and Garrett
Steuart Street Tower, Suite 1300
One Market Plaza
San Francisco, CA  94105-1402

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. A-92-476

Dear Mr. Lipton:

     This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of the Marin Hospital District pursuant to the mass mailing
and other provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTION

     Do the mass mailing and other provisions of the Act prohibit
the distribution of the proposed Annual Report of the Marin
Hospital District either in the form of a mailing sent to district
residents or as an advertisement in a newspaper of general
circulation?

CONCLUSION

     The mass mailing and other provisions of the Act do not
prohibit the distribution of the proposed Annual Report of the
Marin Hospital District in the form of a mailing provided that it
does not feature, or otherwise include the name, office,
photograph, or any other reference to the hospital district board


_________________________

1    Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800  •  P.O. Box 807  •  Sacramento, CA  95812  •  (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-92-476
Page 2


members. Moreover, the Act does not prohibit the publication of
the annual report as an advertisement in a newspaper, provided
that the newspaper is not published by the hospital district and
is a newspaper of general circulation.

FACTS

     The Marin Hospital District is considering the distribution
at public expense of an "Annual Report to the Community." The
report will either be mailed to all residents of the district or
published as an advertisement or insert in one or more local
newspapers within the district. The final version of the report
will not contain the names or photographs of the members of the
district board. It will be accompanied by a cover letter which
will generally describe the contents of the report. The draft of
the report makes references to the Marin Hospital District and the
district board, but does not make individual reference to any of
the board members of the Marin Hospital District.

ANALYSIS

Mass Mailing Prohibition

     In June 1988, Proposition 73 amended Section 89001 of the Act
to provide: "No newsletter or other mass mailing shall be sent at
public expense." The Commission adopted Regulation 18901 to
clarify which mass mailings were permissible and which were
prohibited under the Act. Regulation 18901(a) provides that a
mailing is prohibited only if all of the following apply:

     (1) Any item sent is delivered, by any means,
to the recipient at his or her residence, place of
employment or business, or post office box. For
purposes of this subdivision (a)(1), the item
delivered to the recipient must be a tangible item,
such as a videotape, record, or button, or a
written document.

     (2) The item sent either:

          (A) Features an elected officer
     affiliated with the agency which produces
     or sends the mailing, or

          (B) Includes the name, office,
     photograph, or other reference to an
     elected officer affiliated with the
     agency which produces or sends the mail-
     ing, and is prepared or sent in
     cooperation, coordination,
     or concert with the elected officer;

     (3)(A) Any of the costs of distribution
is paid for with public moneys; or

Our File No. A-92-476
Page 3

(B) Costs of design, production,
and printing exceeding $50.00 are paid
with public moneys, and the design,
production, or printing is done with the
intent of sending the item other than as
permitted by this regulation.

(4) More than two hundred substantially
similar items are sent, in a single calendar
month, excluding any item sent in response to
an unsolicited request and any item described
in subdivision (b).

Regulation 1890l(a).

The Marin Hospital District members are local elected
officials. Thus, they are public officials subject to the
provisions of Section 89001. (Section 82048.)

1. Transmission

Regulation 1890l(a)(1) only restricts items that are mailed
or delivered, by any means, to a person's home, office or post
office box. If items are set out for the public to pick up on
their own, or are handed out in a public area, the restrictions of
the regulation do not apply.

According to the facts you have provided, the district may
decide to send its annual report directly to district residents.
If the reports are delivered to district residents, by any means,
to a person's home, office, or post office box, the reports will
be transmitted as set forth in Regulation 1890l(a)(1).

2. Inclusion of a Reference to an Elected Officer

If the item does not feature or include the name, office,
photograph or any reference to an elected officer who is
affiliated with the agency that produces or distributes the
mailing, the item is not subject to the restrictions of the
regulation. (Regulation 1890l(a)(2).) An elected officer is
"affiliated" with an agency if the officer is a member, officer or
employee of the agency or a subunit (such as a committee) of that
agency; has supervisory control over the agency; or appoints any of
the members of the agency. (Regulation 1890l(c)(1); Bidie Advice
Letter, No. A-89-096.) Furthermore, if a mailing has been prepared
or sent in cooperation, consultation, coordination or concert with
the elected officer, any use of the elected officer's name,
photograph or office, or any reference to the office is
prohibited. (Regulation 1890l(a)(2)(B).)

The draft of the proposed annual report which you have
provided does not include individual references to the elected
officials, but does include general references to the district or

Our File No. A-92-476
Page 4


the board. We have advised that a general reference to "board" is
not a reference to individual board members. (Martin Advice
Letter, A-91-423 and Owen Advice Letter, No. I-90-704.) Thus, a
reference to the Marin Hospital District is not a specific
reference to the elected officials who are district board members.

Since the annual report would not include the name, office,
photograph, or any other reference to the district board members,
the annual report would not be subject to the restrictions of the
regulation.

3. Public Moneys

Section 89001 provides that no newsletter or other mass
mailing shall be sent at public expense. Regulation 18901(a)(3)
specifies that a mass mailing is "sent at public expense" within
the meaning of Section 89001 if either the costs of distribution
are paid for with public moneys² or more than $50.00 in public
money is paid for the costs of design or production, and the design
or printing is done with the intent of sending the item. The
communications in question would be produced and distributed at
public expense.

4. Exception - Unsolicited Requests

Regulation 18901 applies only to a "mass mailing" as defined
in the Act. "Mass mailing" means over two hundred substantially
similar pieces of mail, but does not include mail which is sent in
response to an unsolicited request and any items that fall within
the exceptions set forth in Regulation 18901(b). (Section 82041.5;
Regulation 18901(a)(4).) However, the mass mailing provisions of
the Act do not apply to unsolicited requests. An unsolicited
request is:

A written or oral communication (including a
petition) which specifically requests a response
and which is not requested or induced by the
recipient elected officer or by any third person
acting at his or her behest. However, an
unsolicited oral or written communication (includ-
ing a petition) which contains no specific request
for a response, will be deemed to constitute an
unsolicited request for a single written response.

***


2 Section 85102(e) defines "public moneys" to include all bonds
and evidence of indebtedness, and all moneys belonging to the
state, or any city, county, town, district, or public agency
therein, and all moneys, bonds, and evidence of indebtedness
received or held by state, county, city, district, city, town, or public
agency officers in their official capacity.

Our File No. A-92-476
Page 5


     A person who subscribes to newspapers or other
periodicals published by persons other than elected
offices shall be deemed to have made unsolicited
requests for materials published in those
subscription publications.

               Regulation 18901(c)(4)(A) and (E).

     This exception would not be met if the district decides to
send the annual report to district residents. However, if the
report were published as an advertisement or insert in a newspaper
of general circulation, the exception would be met. A person who
subscribes to a newspaper of general circulation (that is not
published by the hospital district) is deemed to have made
unsolicited requests for materials published in the newspaper.
(Geioque Advice Letter, No. A-91-193 and Weckerle Advice Letter,
No. A-92-464.)

     In summary, on the basis of the information you have
provided, it does not appear that all the criteria of Regulation
18901(a)(1) have been met. Therefore, we conclude, Section 89001
does not prohibit the distribution of the Annual Report of the
Marin Hospital District either in the form of a mailing sent to
district residents or as an advertisement in a newspaper of
general circulation.

     The cover letter which you indicate may accompany the report,
however, must independently satisfy the criteria of Section 89001.
Regulation 18901 sets forth a series of specific exceptions to the
mass mailing prohibition. For example, Regulation 18901(b)(1)
permits inclusion of an elected officer's name in the standard
letterhead or logotype of stationery, forms, and envelopes of
the official's agency, or in a roster listing containing the names
of all elected officers, provided they appear in the same type
size, typeface, type color, and location. However, the cover
letter, may not include an elected officer's photograph,
signature, or any other reference to the elected officer, except
as specifically permitted by another exception under subsection
(b) of Regulation 18901. This exception is limited to the
inclusion of the board members' name in the letterhead or roster
listing and not in the text itself.

Public Funds Prohibition

     Please note that a mailing disseminated for the purpose of
seeking office and paid for with public funds is prohibited under
Section 85300 of the Act. This section has been interpreted to
prohibit the use of public moneys to advocate or promote a
candidate's election to public office. (Gatling Advice Letter,
No. I-90-048.) It does not appear from the proposed annual report
you have provided that the report advocates the reelection of the
hospital district board members or refers to their candidacy.
Consequently, provided public funds are not otherwise used by the

Our File No. A-92-476
Page 6


district in violation of Section 85300,³ the prohibitions of this
section do not appear to be applicable to this situation.⁴
(Calhoun Advice Letter, A-91-276.)

I trust this letter addresses your concerns. If you have any
further questions regarding this matter, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5901.⁵


Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

[signature]

By: Luisa Menchaca
    Counsel, Legal Division

SH:LM
Enclosures




3        Please refer to the McCarthy Advice Letter, No. I-92-520,
which refers to a different set of facts which may be applicable.
(Copy enclosed.)

4        Nothing in this letter should be construed to be an authoriza-
tion for any specific expenditures made by a state or local
government agency. The Commission cannot determine whether an
expenditure is a legitimate government expenditure. We can only
advise as to whether such expenditures are a violation of the
provisions of the Act. Moreover, other provisions of the Act may
apply to these facts which are outside the jurisdiction of the
Commission. For example, Penal Code Section 424 restricts the use
of public funds for political activities. (See, e.g., Opinion v.
Mott (1976) 17 Cal.3d 206; People v. Sperl (1976) 54 Cal.App.3d
640; and People v. Battin (1978) 77 Cal.App.3d 635.)

5        Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries. Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

sections.question: Do the mass mailing and other provisions of the Act prohibit the distribution of the proposed Annual Report of the Mann Hospital District either in the form of a mailing sent to district residents or as an advertisement in a newspaper of general circulation?
sections.conclusion: The mass mailing and other provisions of the Act do not prohibit the distribution of the proposed Annual Report of the Mann Hospital District in the form of a mailing provided that it does not feature, or otherwise include the name, office, photograph, or any other reference to the hospital district board members. Moreover, the Act does not prohibit the publication of the annual report as an advertisement in a newspaper, provided that the newspaper is not published by the hospital district and is a newspaper of general circulation.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether the Political Reform Act's mass mailing provisions prohibit the Mann Hospital District from distributing an Annual Report to residents via mail or as a newspaper advertisement.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that mass mailing prohibitions do not apply to the Annual Report if it contains no individual references to elected board members. Distribution as a direct mailing to residents or as a newspaper advertisement in a non-district-published newspaper of general circulation is permitted, with the latter qualifying as a response to unsolicited requests (newspaper subscriptions).
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Standard advice letter format with clear QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections. Document includes detailed FACTS and ANALYSIS sections. File reference A-92-476 from October 27, 1992. Minor OCR artifacts present (e.g., 'CONCLUS ION' with space). All substantive content clearly extracted.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Do the mass mailing and other provisions of the Act prohibit the distribution of the proposed Annual Report of the Mann Hospital District either in the form of a mailing sent to district residents or as an advertisement in a newspaper of general circulation?

CONCLUSION: The mass mailing and other provisions of the Act do not prohibit the distribution of the proposed Annual Report of the Mann Hospital District in the form of a mailing provided that it does not feature, or otherwise include the name, office, photograph, or any other reference to the hospital district board members. Moreover, the Act does not prohibit the publication of the annual report as an advertisement in a newspaper, provided that the newspaper is not published by the hospital district and is a newspaper of general circulation.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises that Mann Hospital District may distribute its Annual Report either by mailing to district residents or as a newspaper advertisement without violating mass mailing restrictions under the Political Reform Act, provided the report does not feature or reference individual elected board members by name, photograph, or office.
